name: Florida
scopeNote: Statewide ePortal constraints for Florida trial courts, District Courts of Appeal, and the Florida Supreme Court. Local document-type practices may vary by clerk.
guidanceNote: These checks are guidance only — not legal advice…tell us at support@macrify.me

constraints.label: Letter portrait pages
constraints.authority: Fla. R. Gen. Prac. & Jud. Admin. 2.520

constraints.label: First-page clerk stamp space
constraints.authority: Fla. R. Gen. Prac. & Jud. Admin. 2.520

constraints.label: Searchable text
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

constraints.label: Portal file size cap
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority FAQ for Filers, Nov. 2021
constraints.note: research 2026-07-02: trial-court envelope 50 MB, appellate 200 MB — deliberately kept at conservative 25 MB default; per-run override covers larger chunks

constraints.label: Portal envelope size cap
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority FAQ for Filers, Nov. 2021
constraints.note: research 2026-07-02: trial-court envelope 50 MB, appellate 200 MB — deliberately kept at conservative 25 MB default; per-run override covers larger chunks

constraints.label: Filename length and characters
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

constraints.label: Selected filenames
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

constraints.label: PDF/A preference
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021

constraints.label: Active content
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

constraints.label: Encryption and restrictions
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021

constraints.label: Embedded files
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

constraints.label: Metadata scrub
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021

constraints.label: Flatten forms
constraints.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

phrasesAny: certify that prior to filing this motion
check.passDetail: Certificate of conferral language or a Rule 1.202 citation was found.
check.missingDetail: Certificate of conferral possibly missing — Fla. R. Civ. P. 1.202 requires a certificate (that conferral occurred, or that it is not required) at the end of the motion, above the signature block.
check.noTextDetail: Can't verify — no searchable text; run Make Searchable first.

pdfa.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021
pdfa.note: The ePortal's PDF/A check is informational only; non-conformant files still file. PDF/A-2a is preferred for born-digital documents, while PDF/A-2b is acceptable for scanned documents. Full metadata scrubbing can remove the pdfcreator tag needed by PDF/A conformance checks.

activeContent.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025
activeContent.note: JavaScript and form actions are prohibited PDF/A document-intelligence elements.

encryption.condition: when submitted as PDF/A; encryption-key digital signatures are not passed through by the Portal
encryption.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021

embeddedFiles.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025

metadataScrub.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025; Florida Courts E-Filing Authority PDF/A FAQ, revised Aug. 2021
metadataScrub.note: Formal standards do not impose a general metadata-scrub requirement, but the Portal PDF/A FAQ describes a conflict: scrubbing the pdfcreator tag can make PDF/A conformance verification fail.

ocr.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025
ocr.note: PDF documents filed with the Portal must be searchable; scanned documents should be OCR'd.

flattenForms.condition: form fields and actions are prohibited when submitted as PDF/A
flattenForms.authority: Florida Courts Technology Standards v4.0, adopted May 2025
